retries.304-etag-update-response-ETag: the origin answers revalidation with a 304 whose ETag does not identify the stored entry; per RFC 9111 the cache must not serve those unvalidated bytes, so RevalidationHandler#retryUnconditional issues one deliberate unconditional refetch — a single intended re-dispatch, not a retry-interceptor bug

setupFailures.headers-store-Content-Range: the test's Content-Range value is not a `bytes …/…` byte-range spec, so parseContentRange rejects it and the 200 is refused storage (skip 'content-range') — it is never cached, so the reuse request misses
setupFailures.headers-store-Set-Cookie: Set-Cookie responses are never stored (shared-cache decision)
setupFailures.304-etag-update-response-Content-Range: the setup 200's Content-Range is not a `bytes …/…` byte-range spec, so it is refused storage (skip 'content-range'); with nothing cached there is nothing to revalidate, so request 2 is unconditional
setupFailures.304-etag-update-response-Set-Cookie: Set-Cookie responses are never stored, so there is nothing to revalidate
setupFailures.head-200-update: HEAD is keyed separately from GET; a HEAD response can't update the GET entry
setupFailures.head-410-update: HEAD is keyed separately from GET; a HEAD response can't update the GET entry
setupFailures.partial-use-headers: the stored entry is a complete 200; this cache does not slice a sub-range out of a wider stored representation, so the Range request is forwarded to the origin instead of being answered from cache
setupFailures.partial-use-stored-headers: the stored entry is a complete 200; this cache does not slice a sub-range out of a wider stored representation, so the Range request is forwarded to the origin instead of being answered from cache
